tasks.title: CC Growth Plan and Buglist
tasks.description: Overview and assignment: We are all capable of identifying customer needs, in part by noting deficiencies in goods and services we use day to day. 1. Bug list component: To prove to yourself that you can identify such needs, please generate a list of at least 20 ‘bugs’ or potential challenges that exist in everyday life. Please upload a completed version of the template here: Overview of the exercise and questions to ask Want to learn / read more about Bug lists? You can check out these resources: Thinkertoys ; Creative Confidence . 2. Creative Confidence growth plan component: Expanding your creative confidence can be as simple as striving to (a) Observe in new ways, (b) Interview someone to ask questions, and (c) Experience something--first person or via video--to gain a new perspective. Simply list several ways you hope to expand your creative confidence by specifying new ways you can observe, interview, and experience new things as part of your everyday life . Here are some examples and additional details from the last time I did this exercise in Luxembourg City, Luxembourg while teaching a workshop. What to learn more? You can reference Kelley & Kelley's material for this week (Intro and parts of Ch. 1 from their book) as they detail ways we can cultivate our creative confidence. This is consistent with the notion of " everyday awe "--seeking daily ways to get outside ourself, increase our curiosity, and find novelty in our daily living. Some growing research ( Williams & Shepherd, 2025 ) reveals the link between awe and transformational opportunities. You can submit your plan in the text response or in a document. Grading Rubric: Grading will primarily be based on: Evidence of sincere effort to identify key problems, NOT the 'monetizability' of the problem. Remember, you're looking for problems, issues, or things that could be fixed, NOT identifying the solution. Problems have many, many solutions, so jumping in too soon misses the exploration. Degree to which you followed instructions Length: Be succinct and focused.

You can submit your plan in the text response or in a document. Grading: Grading will primarily be based on: Evidence of sincere effort to identify key problems The level of detail and effort demonstrated Degree to which you followed instructions

tasks.title: Class Participation (Mid-term)
tasks.description: Participation and professionalism—Posted for mid-term (0-30 points) and term end (0-30 pts) A baseline expectation of this course is that you attend and participate. The ability to ‘think on your feet,’ constructively engage in a debate on class topics, and persuasively and succinctly articulate your point of view is critical for entrepreneurs, product managers and honestly for just about any profession and as a general life skill. What I will be looking for in class discussions is the extent to which what you add to our discussions contributes to the learning of others in the class. If you are by nature a quiet person, please come talk to me and I’ll help bring you into our class discussions.Good comments possess one or more of the following attributes: • Relate to the topic at hand and/or integrate the topic with other course content. • Are succinct and avoid hogging attention, time, or one’s point of view (i.e., you acknowledge others in the conversation). • Offer a different (sometimes contrarian), unique, and relevant perspective on the issue. • Contribute to moving the discussion forward. • Build on but don’t repeat other comments (too often we fail to listen to the comments of others). • Transcend the “I feel” syndrome; that is, they include some evidence or analysis of inherent tradeoffs. In other words, they demonstrate some reflective thinking. • Offer solutions and /or possibilities rather than merely a critique. As the saying goes (in various forms)—“any idiot can tear down a barn, but it takes a carpenter to build one.” Pointing out flaws, errors, and so forth is easy and not that interesting. Offering alternatives that can be critiqued takes effort, courage, and helps move our conversations forward. Assessing participation (5 points each for 11 class sessions) I will grade each student’s contribution immediately following class and will post your score at mid and end of term (i.e., two scores ranging from 0-30 points). If you would like to know the status of your participation score, please come speak with me. Absences, tardiness, and leaving early. Attendance is required for this course, so the absolute floor for participation and professionalism is showing up! I allow 1 excused absence (for any reasons, so use it wisely) throughout the semester as a general policy. If you have unique circumstances—please communicate those as early as possible. Missing classes beyond the 1 excused absence will result in a 3% decrease in your final grade (e.g., 95% --> 92% and so on) . University approved absences and emergencies will be handled on a case-by-case basis. Again, just remember—use your one freebie wisely. Showing up late and/or leaving early is disruptive to your fellow students, teams, and me . While I generally handle these issues on a case-by-case basis, if you are later than 5 minutes to class (without letting me know) and/or leave early (without alerting me to the situation) then you will be marked absent for the day. Daily scoring for professionalism and participation . During each class I will make notes about participation. I will score participation in the following way: You begin each session with a baseline score of 3 out of 5—that is for attending . Your score can then go up or down depending on the professionalism and quality of your participation. Factors decreasing your score . Unprofessional conduct (i.e., demeaning others, hogging the conversation, etc.) and/or unproductive comments (i.e., simply re-stating someone’s remarks to ‘check the box’) can result in your score going down from 3 to 2 or 1. Similarly, inappropriate use of technology (i.e., violating BYU-Marriott’s phone policy ; distracting yourself/others with off-topic computer usage , etc.) will decrease your score. Factors increasing your score . Professional and ‘good comments’ (see above) will result in an increase from the baseline 3 up to 4 or 5. Please do not overly worry about getting a ‘perfect’ participation score . The goal is to cultivate a community of learning where we engage the content together , integrate our understanding with one another, HAVE FUN , and take our learning to the next level . Let’s all commit to creating an environment for this to happen.

Throughout the class I'll be asking you to generate original ideas. Copying or plagiarizing concepts completely misses the point of that exercise.

tasks.title: 📝 Preparation/ Participation - Introduction
tasks.description: Preparation for the Live Class Read the Syllabus Make yourself comfortable with the canvas site Read Bring your breakthrough ideas to life.pdf Ensure you have completed the pre-class materials The goal of these assignments is to (a) get your into the reading, (b) get you to engage the reading, and (c) allow us to be on the same page for class. I want to see genuine engagement with the work. Rubric: Minimum of ~1-2 paragraphs per question response. Please do not use AI for this--I'm looking for your personal reaction and response to the reading. Please do not exceed 1 page. If you do, please justify this decision. Justifications might include: "I was on a roll and was inspired by the reading, so I just had to get it out!" Remember the goal here--I don't want to 'bog you down' but I do want you to engage prior to class. Assignment : Please answer the following questions. You can enter these as text or submit a file (Word document). Have a better idea? Propose it to me and we'll explore! RESPONSE PROMPTS Submit (via text, document upload, or otherwise) responses to the following questions: How do you see innovation being relevant to your future work? Please give examples from the article. What 'gaps' do you see in your current ability to be innovative and how might you make progress toward filling those gaps? What small, simple things do you commit to do during this semester to increase your creativity, innovativeness, and growth mindset? ↩ 🗂 Session 1: Introduction

tasks.title: 📝 Preparation/ Participation
tasks.description: Preparation for the Class Read the Case 'Savannah Bananas.' This case is about innovation and market disruption as viewed through the lens of one of the most entertaining and dynamic sports teams in baseball: the Savannah Bananas. Fans First Entertainment created a modified version of baseball they call "Banana Ball." The goal of this case study is to explore (a) how this innovation emerged, (b) identify potentially repeatable 'common principles' of entrepreneurial innovation, (c) and to consider the roles of diverse stakeholders (the focal organization, customers, existing market competitors/alternatives, baseball players, etc.) in achieving an innovation. In preparation for the case, please answer prepare responses to the following questions (enter responses via text or submit via a file): How are the Savannah Bananas innovating the game of baseball and what other organizations can you identify that are innovative / disruptive in similar ways? When creating something new 'failure' is inevitable. What was SB's approach to failure and why was that important? Please consider examples of how their approach to failing/failure is similar to or different from organizations you've been part of. How did SB prioritize needs of different stakeholder: fans first, investors, other groups? What do you make of their "Five E" leadership principles What role does an organization's culture play in cultivating innovation? What factors do you see as being important or simply idiosyncratic to SB? As time permits--reflect on what SB might do next in expanding its innovative approach to baseball without losing the very value that led to its emergence. Please come prepared ready to engage with your classmates in exploring the material. ↩ 🗂 Session 04 (Contexts, stakeholders, and processes of EI)

tasks.title: 📝 Preparation/ Participation
tasks.description: Preparation for the Live Class Complete the assigned readings listed in this module Answer the following questions (enter answers via text or submit via a file). Please come ready to use the frameworks we read about in our class discussion. What is your understanding of Jobs-to-be-done and when might you see the framework (in full or partial form) as being useful for problem-solving? What connections do you see between: Jobs to be done and Value Proposition Value proposition and principles of iteration Please list 1-2 examples that resonated with you from any of the reading, explaining the principle from the framework that links to the example. Please come prepared ready to engage with your classmates in exploring the material. ↩ 🗂 Session 05: Producing ideas by fulfilling jobs to be done

tasks.title: 📝 Preparation/ Participation
tasks.description: Preparation for the Live Class Complete the assigned readings listed in this module (excerpts from Creative Construction and Safi Bahcall's talk on Loonshots ) Answer the following questions (enter answers via text or submit via a file) 1) Who actually “owns” innovation at different stages—and how does that ownership shift? Drawing on Creative Construction (Chs. 5–6 and 8–10) and Bahcall’s distinction between loonshots and franchises , identify key stakeholders involved at idea creation, validation, scaling, and institutionalization . In considering the question above, you might explore which stakeholders add value at each stage, and which tend to unintentionally suppress innovation? How should a leader change their behavior as ownership shifts (e.g., from explorer to translator to protector)? 2) When does resistance to innovation signal incompetence—and when does it signal responsibility? Both Creative Construction and Bahcall suggest that resistance often comes from rational actors protecting legitimate interests (capital, reputation, customers, employees). To answer the above question you might do the following: Choose one stakeholder group (e.g., middle managers, regulators, incumbent business units, investors). The ask: Under what conditions is their resistance healthy rather than regressive? How should innovators reframe their thinking to collaborate with—not bypass or villainize—this group? 3) What mental model must leaders unlearn to keep loonshots alive without destroying the organization? What deeply held beliefs (e.g., efficiency first, ROI certainty, org charts, 'talent solves everything' etc.) become problematic and even dangerous in attempting to scale innovation? Try and compare and contrast some of the ideas here with other material (Grant readings, etc.). How do we get better at asking / answering 'what is needed here' rather than falling back on assumptions? Please come prepared ready to engage with your classmates in exploring the material. ↩ 🗂 Session 06

tasks.title: 📝 Preparation/ Participation-Section 002
tasks.description: Preparation for the Live Class Please prepare by: Watching the Design Sprint overview video files from Jake Knapp and team (~50 mins--you can watch at double or triple speed). While there have been significant updates to the process since it was released, I find that these original videos are helpful in explaining that gap it was filling. You will notice similarities to Agile and other approaches, albeit with key differences that we will uncover and explore. Watching the interview files (just a quick overview--can dig into this more) from Angel Studios. Please review the PDF file I have shared that gives some overview information. Answer the following questions (enter answers via text or submit via a file) What are the features of situations / problems that are best addressed with the Sprint process as opposed to other decision-making frameworks? Please document a list of ideas (start with 3-5, but don't stop if you feel inspired) you believe represent the problems Angel Studios is facing? Don't focus on solutions, just try and build out / document problems at this point. Please come prepared ready to engage with your classmates in exploring the material. ↩ 🗂 Session 07

tasks.title: 📝 Preparation/ Participation-Section 02
tasks.description: Preparation for the Live Class Building off our overview class (previous session), please come prepared with the following so you can jump in as a team during class: Individually consider the following about Angel Studios and submit some brief notes on the following. Remember, this is problem definition, not solutioning . Your notes will be used in class for the Map and Sketch stages of the Design Sprint: Their core business model and value proposition (you can draw on prior frameworks for this, including the business model canvas ) to help you think about imagine different problem fields across stakeholders. Key stakeholders and what each is optimizing for? What are the 'jobs they need done' etc. 2–4 major problems or tensions the company is facing--what do you feel are the most important issues they should tackle in the next 6 months to a year? Turn problems into questions: Ask 'how my we' questions (as many as you'd like!) to try and identify the most pressing questions Angel should tackle next . Please submit this prior to class and bring a copy to refer to during team work. Please come prepared ready to engage with your classmates in exploring the material. ↩ 🗂 Session 08

tasks.title: Angel Case Materials posted
tasks.description: Hi all, I wanted to make sure you see the content I have posted from Angel Studios. They have now given me more feedback on the specific things they're wrestling with. I'll paste this here but direct your attention to 🗂 Session 7: (🗓 Feb 3)-Sprint and Angel Overview (Short videos for prep) where I have these details posted. Thank you all, have a great weekend. ------ Here are two short videos Angel Studios shared with us from some internal interviews. They posted these on Youtube for us. I also have included other materials you can begin exploring. 1. Entrepreneurial journey: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=VtlY19iUE0Q&t=1261s 2. Neal's background: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XeDDVBAEq4o&t=82s 3. Review Angel's Website: https://www.angel.com/guild/join?origin=home ; Please note the different genres, audiences, and content they have in pursuit of telling 'stories that matter' as their core value proposition. https://www.angel.com/blog/angel-studios/posts/angel-lights-up-cinemacon-2025-with-bold-new-slate PRIMARY CASE PROMPT: Key challenge and questions facing Angel Studios. Here are the key questions / principles Angel Studios is striving to answer: Angel wants to help viewers find what they want, when they want it, in entertainment situations that go beyond being "merely a Sunday-only brand." While telling stories that are values-based (i.e., they matter because they "amplify light") , they want to continue engaging their guild in appropriate ways. Given that, the primary questions they want to answer are: How do we solve the recommendation engine problem? As our library continues to expand, how do we continue to give the user a good experience? A few points to keep in mind as you explore the questions: (1) Angel doesn't want to replace the responsibility of parents to vet content; It's about a co-viewing experience. (2) Co-creation involves engaging the guild ; This is for content creation, vetting, and production. (3) Angel has people who enjoy Hallmark-type films, and people who are more cinephiles who don't like the Hallmark-type, and vice versa. How do they ensure a good experience for the diverse audiences we serve? For example, Hallmark, Christian and "Sunday," and so forth that fall under the 'values-based brand' we're pursuing. As you watch these, review the questions, and begin studying more about Angel Studios, please begin thinking about potential problem domains you might consider tackling as a team for your projects.

tasks.title: Another Guest Opportunity--Mark Ballif (Plum Healthcare)
tasks.description: Hi all, Given the interest I saw from our class in our last guest, I wanted to share with you that I will have another amazing guest coming to my Wednesday New Venture Launchpad Class this upcoming Wednesday (3:30-4:45 in TNRB 240). Mark Ballif and his team at Plum Healthcare were responsible for transforming the Nursing Home industry in California several years back. Mark has a wealth of knowledge on topics ranging from (a) identifying opportunities in difficult industries, (b) turning organizations around, and (c) teaming up with every member of your organization to achieve positive outcomes for the venture, customer, investor, and team member / employee. He brings a unique perspective to topics of entrepreneurial innovation especially as it relates to the "Entrepreneurship Through Acquisition" approach. I have asked Mark to focus on the following primary topics : I have asked Mark to share experiences about what the situation was with Plum when he started, how doing things small and large in an iterative way was so helpful, and what he might recommend you do in beginning your entrepreneurial but also life journeys. Why is it that others ‘missed’ the opportunity Plum fulfilled? And how have things gone since selling—have they maintained their ethos? How important is it to have a ’thought partner’ to help develop, revise, and implement ideas? How to cultivate curiosity, understanding, and exploration with other people if I struggle with it. Navigating team-based collaborations—how to get the most out of them Surprising partnerships (legal examples, etc.) and how doing/being consistent with your character was so valuable. Building and proliferating a culture (or non-culture, culture) of building good connections in an organization—“the culture is a product of everyone’s engagement not something that is ‘done’ to them.” While certainly not required, you are invited to attend if you would like. MARK BALLIF BIO (From LinkedIn): I have over 30 years of executive experience building enterprises with intense regulatory and people demands. I co-founded Plum Healthcare Group in 1999 and served as Co-CEO through 2014 and executive board member until 2020. From 2015-2019, I served as a managing partner at Granite Development, LLC, a real estate development affiliate of Plum. Today, I shepherd portfolio companies in the fuel processing, energy wastewater treatment, and creative services sectors. My board experience includes Bay Bridge Capital Partners, Plum Healthcare Group, the Arbinger Institute, EcoNova, BiofilCom, a sanitation venture between the Bill and Melinda Gates Foundation and Biofil Corporation in Accra, Ghana, Rise and Rebuild Foundation, Trustee for the Pacific Ridge School, and a member of Bain & Company’s Merger Integration Advisory Board. Julia and I served as mission leaders of the Philippines Manila Mission for The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints (2020-2023). I hold a BA in Philosophy and an MA in International and Area Studies from Brigham Young University.

tasks.title: Initial assignments posted--Guidance moving forward
tasks.description: I all, I hope you're having a great weekend! You'll see I posted grades for week 1. Thank you for engaging with our work, keeping up on things, and for your sincere efforts in striving to improve during this course. I'm thrilled with your willingness to engage, learn, and grow--that makes teaching so much fun. A few thoughts moving forward. 1. General vs. specific. We've talked about tensions. One I have is general vs. specific feedback. On the one hand, I could spend hours on each one of your responses. However, then you would get your results/ grades sometime next year! So I have to try and sacrifice specifics for speed. I don't like that but it is a reality. That said--if you want more direct / specific feedback on something in particular then please call it out in your submission and/or via email to me. As always, I'm happy to meet too if that is a better setting. 2. Moving forward. Moving forward, I will differentiate between full points / a few points getting docked primarily on (a) how specific you were in calling out examples, principles, and concepts from the reading and (b) if/how you applied it to your situation and setting. I'm not looking for Master's theses here each week, but rather a more overt effort to ID the key concepts that resonated with you. To be clear, this doesn't need to be all of the concepts in the article and certainly should NOT be a summary of everything. Again, I'm thrilled with the ways you engaged the material and I'm looking forward to seeing how you progress in your goals moving through the semester. Trent

I'll be engaging in this too so we can compare notes as we roll along. Have a great weekend!

tasks.title: Welcome to Entrepreneurial Innovation
tasks.description: Hi all, Welcome to the Entrepreneurial Innovation Class! I'm very excited for this semester. I have taught versions of this class in the past, yet have re-tooled this class to better meet the needs of you as students. Brief Introduction First, my name is Trent Williams and I am the instructor for your Entrepreneurial Innovation class. We'll be getting to know one another a lot better over the coming weeks, but I do want to quickly introduce myself. I joined BYU in the summer of 2023. Prior to coming to BYU I was a faculty member at the Kelley School of Business (Indiana University) for 6 years and the Whitman School of Management (Syracuse University) for 2 years. Both of those schools have very strong ENT programs and I’ve loved spending my entire academic career in the entrepreneurship space. Before beginning a career in Academia (MS and PhD from IU-Kelley), I received an Masters from Purdue's Krannert School of Management and then worked as a management consultant focused on strategy and corporate entrepreneurship (PwC Advisory and Deloitte Consulting). Prior to that I helped launch a boutique start-up consulting firm that is still operational and focuses on helping clients develop experience strategies when designing new products, services, and customer interactions. I have taught MBA, executive MBA, undergrad, and corporate clients courses and seminars including Strategic Renewal, Corporate Innovation, New Venture Creation, Innovation and Design Sprints, Strategic Management, and so forth. I have also taught undergraduate courses on similar topics. Much of my time is also spent doing research, which you can read about here if you're having a hard time sleeping! My research focus is on new venture creation, psychology and entrepreneurship--resilience, wellbeing, decision-making--and resourcefulness, among many other topics. Course Overview and Important information 1. Course syllabus and relevant dates. As we prepare for next week I wanted to send out the syllabus (see attached) as well as some announcements. Please treat this syllabus as a general overview. Specific materials, changes, and so forth will be in Canvas. Therefore, I encourage you to set up notifications and reminders so you won't miss anything. Please read the syllabus carefully--including policy on preparation, engagement, participation, late work, and absences. We will discuss more on Tuesday. 2. Pre-class assignments: Prior to the first day of class (January 13) I ask that you complete all of the pre-work in Canvas. This includes pre-class surveys as well as reading / reading submissions . For this class to function you must come prepared each class so we can engage together, integrate information, and learn / grow together. 3. Course expectations. tasks.title: Assignment - KPIs & Power BI Drone Analysis
tasks.description: [Note: do this assignment on your own, don't wait for in-class time to start. Instructions and due date are now updated.] Situation: A commercial drone manufacturer (DronR) introduced a consumer drone that allows parents to track their children. The product has been in the market for the 3 years and the marketing group wants a dashboard to track product performance. Although sales have trended upward, profitability has remained flat and may be increasing in variability. The company needs to improve profitability. Download the following dataset and import it into Power BI: dronr-data.xlsx Step 1: Explore the data in Power BI. Step 2: Create a very short deck reporting on the following: Why isn't profitability increasing? Make a Power BI report and test various visualizations to tell the story of why profitability isn't increasing. Show 2-3 of your most useful visuals in 1-2 slides and describe why they are helpful to identify/solve the profitability problem. Keeping your most useful visuals, add 3 KPI's to your Power BI report page. Describe your KPI's and explain why you think they are relevant for the company to track. Take a screenshot of your Power BI report in Edit mode (showing all the viz and data options) and paste it into a slide. Finally, using your Power BI report, create a KPI dashboard that includes: 1. A line chart of Dollar Sales and Profit (by week) 2. The three KPI's you created above 3. Any other data visuals or statistics you think would be relevant for the marketing team Take a screenshot of your KPI dashboard and drop it into the last slide of your report. Upload your completed deck as a PPTX or PDF.

tasks.title: Assignment - Data Coding Techniques
tasks.description: Download Data Science Salaries.xlsx Do analysis in Excel to answer the questions below. To make it easier to grade, please put each question's analysis in its own sheet. Highlight your answer on the sheet. When done, save and submit your workbook here. Q1 1 point Filter to salary in USD, employee residence in US, and company location in US. How many employees are in the filtered, clean data? IMPORTANT: Use the resulting clean data from Q1 as the data you analyze for the remaining questions. Q2 2 points Use a lookup formula and insert an additional column next to Experience Level that has the value labels. Use COUNTA and COUNTIF to create a summary table with the % of employees at each experience level. Q3a 3 points Create a 4x3 table computing the average salary by Experience Level and Company Size. Q3b 2 points Which size company (S, M, or L) has the highest Executive-level salary? The lowest? Q4 5 points Create a new column with all the "Engineer" related Job Titles, labeling non-engineer jobs as "All Other Jobs". Create a table, sorted by count, of all the Engineer jobs. What are the top 3 Engineer jobs? Q5a 3 points Go back to looking at ALL Job Titles (not just Engineers). What salary does an employee need to be in the top 10% of pay? Q5b 2 points Which Job Title is most common among the top 10% paid employees? Q6 2 points Looking only at Entry-level employees, which size company (S, M, or L) allows the highest Remote work? The lowest? Note: for this analysis, just treat Remote Ratio as numeric and compute the average of the column.

tasks.title: Assignment - Regression on Ad Data with LLMs
tasks.description: Do each analysis below and put the results and answers to questions in a short PPT. Start with these steps: Use the Advertising Data Regression.xlsx data file and import it into an LLM Set a random seed of 42 Split data 80/20 for Train/Test SLIDE 1 Train a Linear Regression with Y = Sales and X = TV, Radio, Newspaper. Explore the data and the initial model. Should we transform any variables? Which variables (if any) should we remove through backward selection? Re-run model with any transformations or revised variables selected, if desired. What is the R 2 value for the initial regression vs. final model? Output coefficients in a small chart or table, and a chart of actual vs. predicted Sales. What Sales predictions do we get for the following? TV = 150, Radio = 25, Newspaper = 20 TV = 400, Radio = 10, Newspaper = 0 TV = 10, Radio = 400, Newspaper = 0 SLIDE 2 Now create a Random Forest Regression . Note: No need to do much data coding or manipulation here. What is the R 2 value for the initial regression vs. final model? Output coefficients in a small chart or table, and a chart of actual vs. predicted Sales. What Sales predictions do we get for the following, and how do they compare to the regression model? Comment on any major differences you see. TV = 150, Radio = 25, Newspaper = 20 TV = 400, Radio = 10, Newspaper = 0 TV = 10, Radio = 400, Newspaper = 0 SLIDE 3 Have the LLM recommend other predictive methods that could improve your results. Try a couple. Describe how regression, RF, and one of the other methods compared (e.g., R 2 values, most important predictors). Which would you recommend using and why? SLIDE 4 Use the LLM code to re-run your regression or random forest in Google Colab or Anaconda. Put screenshots of your results and code into this slide (it doesn't need to be beautiful, just enough to confirm you succeeded at running code outside the LLM).

tasks.title: Assignment - Crio Segmentation with LLMs
tasks.description: Purpose: First, get practical experience with cluster analysis. Second, heavily automate the process using LLMs and AI tools to go from data to insights to reports very quickly. Use cluster analysis to create segments for Crio Bru using this data: Crio Segmentation Data.xlsx Follow these steps: Upload the Crio file to your LLM and describe for the LLM what is contained (raw data + data dictionary) Confirm the uploaded data size and do some basic data viz to better understand the data Create K-means clusters with 3-4 segments (have LLM recommend the optimal number; don't forget to set a random seed) Summarize the results of the segmentation in a table (segments in columns, variables in rows) Have LLM provide its own description of each segment Give the segments useful and memorable names (iterate with your LLM) Generate images that represent the segments (using your LLM or taking your descriptions to a different LLM you like better for images) Suppose that Crio can only target 1 segment. Which segment should they pick and why? Use your LLM to outline a short marketing plan for that segment to increase Crio sales, including a creative brief for a social media campaign Use your LLM or another presentation-building AI tool (e.g., Gamma, Beautiful, Tome) to produce a segmentation report...you will need to summarize your analysis (segment names, descriptions, etc.) then do some minor slide clean-up, including a few key images/outputs from your analysis in the report...aim for 2-3 slides summarizing segments side-by-side, then 1-2 deep-dive slides on each segment (~10 slides max) This assignment is all about combining your own analysis intuition and iterating with LLMs. Apply what you have learned in class but lean heavily on automation for this assignment, do not spend a lot of time manually building the report or doing analysis outside of your LLM!

tasks.title: Assignment - Text Analytics Eco Flower CX
tasks.description: Analyze Customer Experience text data from Eco Flower in an LLM: Eco Flower Customer Experience.xlsx Create a short 1-page document of recommendations with some supporting data/visuals/quotes based on the following analysis: Word clouds of positive (like) and negative (need to improve) comments. Find themes from both like + improve comments. Quantify the prevalence of the themes. Save them as new variables. Predict Likelihood to Recommend (the 0-10 scale) using these new theme variables (as dummy-coded predictors). Combining the analyses above, what recommendations would you make to Eco Flower? Come up with your own recommendations first. Then iterate with your LLM to dive deeper. Use the LLM to get example supporting quotes for the report, e.g., a quote or two from very pleased and very displeased customers to illustrate your recommendations. Find strong quotes with emotion, you might need to iterate with the LLM to get good candidates.

tasks.title: Group Project - Swire Coca-Cola Employee Retention
tasks.description: Analysis setup: Recent economic factors have made the job market more competitive for several roles at Swire Coca-Cola, including Merchandisers, Order Builders, and Drivers. From experience, managers know that employees are more likely to stay with the company for a long time if they stay past 90 days, and our data confirms this. Swire Coca-Cola seeks to provide new employees the proper support and training to ensure their first 90 days on the job are rewarding and successful. Use the dataset Swire Employee Retention Data.xlsx provided to build a predictive model forecast the likelihood that an employee will stay at least 90 days. Each team member should work on the analysis independently, then the group came come together to create a final approach. Create a short PPT report answering the questions below: Slide 1: Try a variety of models to find the most predictive model. Report each model's accuracy and AUC Which model would you select as the best one to use and why? Slide 2: How well does each factor help in predicting employees staying with the organization during their first 90 days? Create visual(s) clearly showing your findings. Slide 3: Which one variable do you recommend Swire focus on to increase the likelihood of employee retention for the first 90 days? Give some thought to causality for this question. What are things they can do that will increase the likelihood of retention? Create a compelling case using any combination of words, visuals, simulations, etc. based on your model output.

tasks.title: Group Project - Employment Conjoint Analysis
tasks.description: Use the following conjoint simulator/analysis and data for this assignment: https://byu.pdx1.qualtrics.com/conjoint-simulator/shared/SV_9WGBhe1eC7kAmma Note there are two pairs subgroups available to analyze, 1) Need Sponsorship vs. US Worker and 2) Female vs. Male Reminder: when you leave the simulator, it will usually reset to defaults when you return, so keep track of what you configure Employment Conjoint Setup and Results.xlsx Note this outlines the conjoint setup, contains the raw utilities for each respondent, and includes a few survey variables than can be used to explore the results Investigate the questions below as individuals for a short time , then come together as a group to provide answers in a PPT with clear supporting data/charts. Create one slide per question. Use Conjoint Analysis and Utility Scores: Are there notable differences in what Females vs. Males find valuable in a job offer? Use Conjoint Analysis, Total Population, and Willingness to Pay: Which 3 attributes seem most valuable to upgrade from the base level offering? Use Conjoint Simulator, Total Population, and Preference Share: Do not alter Job 1 or Job 2 but add Job 3 to the configuration. Design Job 3 in any way you want, subject to these constraints: It must be Visa Sponsored Base Salary + other Costs cannot exceed $130,000 Minimize the % of people who choose None of the job offers (i.e., maximize the sum of Job 1 + Job 2 + Job 3) Stay in the Conjoint Simulator and keep the new configuration with Job 3 you just created: Compare the preferences of Need Sponsorship, Females, and Males for Job 1 vs. Job 2 vs. Job 3. Are there notable differences? What principles would you recommend the HR team follow when they design job offers for a diverse set of candidates? Use Conjoint Simulator, Total Population, and Preference Share: Assume HR policy requires that we offer a single package to all candidates. Delete Job 2 and Job 3. What configuration maximizes the share of Job 1 while keeping its Base Salary + other Costs at $135,000 or less. tasks.title: Case Writeup #1: Freemark Abbey Winery
tasks.description: Prior to the start of class, please upload an answer via Canvas to the following questions. You may work in groups, although the final submission should be your own work. Remember, you are not allowed to use generative AI in any way on case writeup assignments. These case assignments are developed to help you foster your strategic thinking skills, and the process is more important than the outcome (i.e., did you get the "right" answers). Embrace the process and have a long view as you work through this and future cases! 1. Assuming Mr. Jaeger chooses to harvest the Riesling grapes before the storm arrives, how much money will he make? Quantify this as clearly as possible. 2. Assuming Mr. Jaeger chooses to leave the grapes on the vine, what is the probability that the grapes will end up with botrytis, and how much money will he make if that occurs? 3. Imagine you a consultant for Mr Jaeger, brought on to advise him in light of the impending storm. What would you advise Mr Jaeger to do? Justify your answer as strongly as possible.

tasks.title: Case Writeup #3: RyanAir
tasks.description: Prior to the start of class, please upload an answer via Canvas to the following questions. You may work in groups, although the final submission should be your own work. 1. Assume you are a consultant brought in to advise the Ryan brothers on their entry strategy. Do you think it is likely to succeed? Why or why not? 2. Can the Ryan brothers make profits at the 98 Irish pound fare they propose? Support your answer using quantitative cost analysis analysis while specifying carefully your assumptions. 3. How do you expect Aer Lingus and British Airways to respond to RyanAir's entry? Why?** **For this final question, utilize the Competitive Response Profile framework found HERE Download HERE . This is a framework used to systematically think through 1) what drives a competitor, 2) what is the competitor doing (or able to do), and 3) the likely response of a competitor to the moves of another competitor (in this case a potential competitor). This tool can be used when there are a few strong competitors and likely competitive response is key to anticipate. Create a Competitive Response Profile for both Aer Lingus and British Airways to help answer this question.

tasks.title: Case Writeup #4: Cola Wars
tasks.description: The Five Forces Framework is used to assess the structural attractiveness of an industry from the perspective of an incumbent. For this case writeup submission, use the "five forces tool" found under course content or HERE to conduct a 5 forces analysis of the consumer soft drink (CSD producer) industry . Note that this does NOT include the bottlers--just for Coke, Pepsi, and their competitors (the CSD industry). Fill in the initial boxes and each of the lines/ratings (wherever possible). Feel free to adjust the weighting on certain dimensions if you think something is more/less important. After completing this framework, what is your overall assessment about the attractiveness of this industry? Justify your answer. What forces (if any) are particularly concerning and what are the major drivers? Which are not concerning? Now, use the "five forces tool" to conduct a 5 forces analysis of the bottling industry (try to do this one more quickly--we will cover it in less depth in class). After completing this framework, what is your overall assessment about the attractiveness of this industry? Why do you think bottling has historically been a less attractive industry than CSD producing? The PESTLE analysis is used to think about potential futures that might influence an industry's profitability or a company's ability to compete in that industry. Using this framework, and the information presented in the case, what factor(s) do you see that are particularly concerning for CSD producers looking forward?

tasks.title: Case Writeup #5: RoboTech--Storming the US Market
tasks.description: Prior to the start of class, please upload an answer via Canvas to the following questions. You may work in groups, although the final submission should be your own work. 1) With the information available to Chen in 2009, do you think her decision to invest $45 million to diversify RoboTech into medical robotics was appropriate? Why or why not? 2) Do a resource/capability audit for Robotech, as well as an activity audit (firm value chain). What resources, capabilities, and activities are important for them to succeed? 3) How would you evaluate the company's first three years in the US medical device market? What do you think it has done most effectively? Where has it fallen short? 4) What should Chen do now? How should the company move forward into 2017 and beyond? Which, if any of the proposed capital investments should it make?

tasks.title: Case Writeup #6: Aldi--the Dark Horse Discounter
tasks.description: This case writeup has you collect and analyze outside data to better understand Aldi's strategy and the strength of its competitive positioning. If you don't do the case writeup, then just look through to get a strong sense of the pricing and reviews. 1) Create a shopping basket for 5-7 common grocery items. Then compare pricing to buy this basket at both Aldi and Walmart. Report your findings in a table. Note that you can find and use “weekly ads” from Walmart and Aldi posted on their websites. You can also visit Walmart or Walmart.com to compare prices. Finding items first in Aldi’s ad, and then looking them up on Walmart.com (after selecting your store) may be quickest. My favorite Aldi store is in zip code 92508. Of course, make sure to use a consistent zip code in your pricing analysis. Now check out 10 or so online reviews (use Google) for an Aldi store in the United States. What are consistent themes you find in the Aldi experience at that store? What do customers like about Aldi and what tradeoffs do they note in shopping at Aldi vs other grocers? What does your analysis say about Aldi’s value stick relative to Walmart? 2) Put together a comparative cost analysis for Walmart vs Aldi. Does Aldi have a competitive advantage or disadvantage relative to Walmart? 3) Articulate Aldi's strategy. 4) Given your above analyses, should Walmart be worried about Aldi? Should Aldi be worried about Walmart? 5) Do you think Aldi should locate its stores close to Walmart stores, or further away? Why?

tasks.title: Case Writeup #7: Ducati
tasks.description: Please respond to the following case preparation questions for our discussion on Ducati: 1) How did Ducati become the second most profitable motorcycle maker in the world despite its small scale? What is the fundamental logic of Minoli's turnaround? 2) After you watch the video on constructing a strategy canvas (on learnstrategy.byu.edu), create a strategy canvas that shows value curves for Ducati pre-turnaround, Ducati post-turnaround, Harley, and Honda (you can lump other Japanese manufacturers with Honda). What does this highlight about the strategic shift by Minoli? 3) What are the key tangible differentiators and the key intangible differentiators for Ducati? How are these built over time? 4) Can Ducati sustain its position in the sport segment? Can Honda and the other Japanese manufacturers stop its growth in this segment? 5) Imagine you are a consultant hired by Ducati in 2001 to help it grow and achieve its goals. What strategic alternative do you recommend Minoli should pursue in 2001, and why?

tasks.title: Final Group Project Team Contract
tasks.description: A team contract is an agreement between you and your teammates about how your team will operate during this course. It outlines the agreed upon rules and standards that will govern your interactions. It sets ground rules for participation, communication, conflict resolution, decision-making, meeting frequency and expectations, client interaction, etc. It also outlines what "success" looks like for the team, and how team contributions should be assessed at the end of the semester. Please draft and submit a team contract. Below are some areas and questions to consider. Your contract does not have to include all the areas below (this is YOUR team after all), but remember that a skimpy contract will have limited power when problems arise. Goals What are the goals of the team? What are your personal goals for this assignment? What kind of obstacles might you encounter in reaching your goals? What happens if all of you decide you want to get an A grade, but because of time constraints, one person decides that a B will be acceptable? Is it acceptable for one or two team members to do more work than the others in order to get the team an A? Will the team follow a more independent process (division of labor) or work in a more collaborative mode? What should the timing be of key deliverables? Meeting Norms Do you have a preference for when meetings will be held? Do you have a preference for where/how they should be held? What if a member cannot attend? How often do you think the team will need to meet outside of class? How long do you anticipate meetings will be? How will you record and distribute the minutes and action lists produced by each meeting? Work Norms How much time per week do you anticipate it will take to make the project successful? How will work be divided among team members? How will deadlines be set? How will you decide who should do which tasks? Where will you record who is responsible for which tasks? What will happen if someone does not follow through on a commitment (e.g., missing a deadline, not showing up to meetings)? How will the work be reviewed? What happens if people have different opinions on the quality of the work? What will you do if one or more team members are not doing their share of the work? How will you deal with different work habits of individual team members (e.g., some people like to get assignments done as early as possible; others like to work under the pressure of a deadline)? Decision Making Do you need consensus (100% approval of all team members) before making a decision? What will you do if one of you fixates on a particular idea? How will you handle disagreements? Who will be in charge of which aspects of the team? Client Interaction What rules will dictate interactions with your client? Who will be the main point of contact for the team with the client? Communication How can you ensure positive and efficient communication between meetings? How can you ensure positive and efficient communication in meetings? How often do you expect to communicate? What types of communication are out of bounds? What norms and conventions will you adopt to make sure everyone is heard? Evaluation To get a perfect peer contribution score from each team member, what is required? What feedback mechanisms will you adopt to help team members know how they are doing? What behaviors clearly reduce the peer contribution score?

tasks.title: Strategy and your life journal entry #3
tasks.description: The Lord told Joseph Smith, “For all have not every gift given unto them; for there are many gifts, and to every man is given a gift by the Spirit of God. “To some is given one, and to some is given another, that all may be profited thereby.” ( D&C 46:11–12 Links to an external site. .) In a parable the Savior told of a man who was preparing to go to a far country. Before leaving he entrusted to one servant five talents, to another two talents, and to another one talent. The first two servants invested their talents and doubled them. The last servant fearfully buried his one talent in the earth. Upon returning, the traveler said to each of the first two servants, “Well done, thou good and faithful servant,” but to the servant who had buried his talent he said: “Thou wicked and slothful servant, thou knewest that I reap where I sowed not, and gather where I have not strawed: “Thou oughtest therefore to have put my money to the exchangers, and then at my coming I should have received mine own money with usury. “Take therefore the talent from him, and give it unto him which hath ten talents. “And cast ye the unprofitable servant into outer darkness: there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.” ( Matt. 25:26–28, 30 Links to an external site. .) What unique resources and capabilities do you possess, that can provide unique value in your sphere of influence? Are they VRIO? What could you do more to organize around them to exploit in the future (the "O" in VRIO)?

tasks.title: Strategy and your life journal entry #4
tasks.description: Throughout the semester we have seen that strategy is about tradeoffs. It is about choosing what to do, but also about choosing what NOT to do. Leaders in organizations consequently have to have careful "no" criterion set so that they focus on what is most important. In corporate strategy we have seen that it is easy to say YES, to a merger and acquisition based on only limited synergies. Or YES to vertically integrating when there is little rationale for doing so. In business strategy we saw that is is easy to say YES to trying to serve all customer needs or customers, or to trying to delivering value in all ways. But strategy is fundamentally about saying NO to the right things. Ponder on what your "no" criterion are in your life. That is, if something doesn't satisfy X, Y, and/or Z I choose to say NO so that I can improve my performance in other areas that are meaningful for my life strategy.

I hope each of you had a restful and rejuvenating holiday break, and that you are ready for a new semester. I am excited to meet each of you! Business strategy is a fascinating topic, and I’m enthusiastic for the journey ahead. In preparation for tomorrow’s session, please spend some time looking through the course on Canvas and reading through the syllabus. My goal is for this class to provide a transformational experience for all involved, and to be high value add regardless of the functional area you care about in business. You will see in my course learning objectives that this includes transformation on many dimensions—including growing in faith and character. To achieve this lofty goal, it requires all of us working together as a team to learn, grow, and to create a unique environment of mutual respect and belonging. I have sent this email to the master list from the BYU registrar for the class, as well as through Canvas directly. IF YOU DID NOT RECEIVE IT TWICE, it means that you need to update your Canvas notification settings. This could include adding in the correct email you want to use for notifications, or specifying how/when notifications should be sent to you under your Canvas account. I tend to send many notifications, reminders, and thoughts about class throughout the semester, so please make sure to set the correct notification settings now. Here is a link that can help you through this process. I have also provided Canvas helps in the course materials on our Canvas course site. If you haven’t engaged with cases before, I have also included a resource on Canvas under course content called “How to Analyze a Strategy Case?” As this is a case-based class, I want to help you to have the best possible learning experience. Have a great first day of classes, and I look forward to meeting you all in person tomorrow! Cheers, Prof. (Tim) Gubler

tasks.description: Overview To help you better understand your unique talents and how to leverage them in your professional and academic life, you will be completing the CliftonStrengths Assessment (formerly StrengthsFinder) by Gallup. Unlike traditional personality tests, this assessment identifies your natural patterns of thinking, feeling, and behaving. Instructions 1. Purchase the Assessment: Visit the Gallup Strengths Center to purchase your access code. • Recommended Option: **CliftonStrengths 34 ($60)**. While there is a Top 5 option ($25), I strongly recommend the Full 34 report. It provides a complete breakdown of your talent profile and more in-depth reporting on how to manage your "blind spots." 2. Take the Assessment: Set aside about 45 minutes of uninterrupted time. The test is timed, so ensure you are in a quiet environment where you can focus. 3. Download Your Reports: Once finished, please download your "CliftonStrengths 34 Report" and the "Signature Themes Report."

tasks.title: Clifton Strengths Assessments
tasks.description: Hi Team! We are still short by 3 assessments. If you want to participate (and want full participation points), please get your Clifton Strengths assessment submitted today!

tasks.title: Positive/Negative Personal Narratives
tasks.description: WRITE: Limit (500) words on each side of the page -- Your POSITIVE Personal Narrative emphasizing and spinning your strengths. -- On the reverse side of the page, write your NEGATIVE Personal Narrative describing background, describing mistakes, admitting weaknesses, etc.

tasks.title: Worst Advice Ever
tasks.description: Please consider who and when you were given strong career or personal advice, and the consequences to you of listening to or accepting that advice. Q: What is the absolute worst-ever advice you have ever been given and what was the result? On the whole, reflecting back on it, was it generally a positive or negative value to you? NOTE FORMAT: Single-spaced, 1-2 page submission, 10-point Arial font with maximum 1" margins.

tasks.title: I AM / I AM NOT a VICTIM
tasks.description: Please write on one side of a page reasons you ARE a victim entitled to special treatment, and on the other, how you're NOT. On the whole, reflecting on your views, how do you believe they have affected your life? NOTE FORMAT: Single-spaced, 1-2 page submission, 10-point Arial font with maximum 1" margins.

tasks.title: QUIZ 1 - ETA
tasks.description: Answer all questions in (20) minutes.

tasks.title: Quiz 2 - ETA
tasks.description: Answer all of the following. You'll have 15 minutes to complete the quiz.

tasks.title: Quiz 3
tasks.description: You will have ~20 minutes to complete the quiz.

tasks.title: QUIZ 4
tasks.description: Twenty minutes for 20 questions, with varied points totaling 50.

tasks.title: READ and PREPARE: Yale University Investments Office, Nov 2020 (Packet)
tasks.description: How has the Investment Office selected, compensated, and controlled private equity fund managers? What explains the differences between its strategies in private equity with that in other asset classes (e.g., real estate)? How has the Investment Office decided when to make private equity investments? What explains the differences between the strategies in private equity with those in other asset classes? How has the Investment Office made international private equity investments? What explains the differences between the performance of its international and domestic private equity investments? How is the private equity industry changing? How could Swensen’s private equity strategy go wrong? Should David Swensen shift his private equity strategy?

tasks.title: Venture Capital Method – Valuation Problem Set (Packet)
READ and PREPARE: Tad O’Malley, May 2008 (Packet)
tasks.description: Review the case and the three PowerPoint presentations. As you read over the materials, consider how O’Malley should structure his presentation at the partners’ meeting. The following are among the issues you may wish to consider: Which company should he recommend? Which uncertainties should he highlight? Are there valuation issues that he should point out? What organizational issues are likely to influence the partners’ decision ASSIGNMENT: Choose one deal on Tad’s behalf and prepare a set of three PowerPoint slides that he should use when presenting to the general partners. Students will make a presentation in class. – BE PREPARED! SUBMIT: Hard copy of the PPT slides at the beginning of class.

tasks.title: READ and PREPARE: Portfolio and Partnership (Packet)
tasks.description: Did Big Sur Ventures do anything wrong? How could they have avoided the situation expressed in Exhibit 1? What does risk mean in the context of a venture capital firm? For example, is risk in any way tied to the public markets? What dimensions constitute meaningful diversification to a venture capital firm, especially an early-stage venture firm? Geography? Sector? Stage? Which partner is managing which part of the portfolio? What mechanisms (organizational, administrative, or process based) would you suggest Big Sur Ventures implement to manage portfolio risk? What would be the cost? In the current Big Sur, how is the relationship of the partners key to portfolio management?

tasks.title: Submit Sandbox Project
tasks.description: Submit a hard and electronic copy of your slides, a two-page summary of the due diligence analysis, customer calls, valuation workup, etc. Attach supporting documents of work conducted as appendix material. * SANDBOX PROJECT MATERIALS TO BE DELIVERED TO TEAMS BY 3/18 *

tasks.title: READ and PREPARE Case: Brazos Partners and Cheddar’s Inc. (Packet)
tasks.description: What is Brazos’ investment strategy? Does it seem well suited for its position as a first-time fund? How do you assess the merits of the GTI transaction? How has the current recessionary climate affected Brazos’ investment strategy in both favorable and unfavorable ways? Is Cheddar’s an attractive investment? Did Brazos underpay, overpay, or get it just right in their initial investment? What are your major concerns with the proposed deal? Should Brazos allow the company to sell the managers some stock? Is the real estate subsidiary a good idea? If the managers buy more stock, what is the appropriate price?

tasks.title: Final Deliverables for Project
tasks.description: Power point and investment memo. Hard copies.

tasks.title: Valuing New Businesses by Hal Heaton
tasks.description: Attached is the document necessary for upcoming classes. Please let me know if you have any questions.

tasks.title: J-Curve Reading
tasks.description: Attached is the J-Curve reading for next week. Thank you all for making class so enjoyable thus far.

tasks.title: Yale Case
tasks.description: Hello all, Due to issues the bookstore is experiencing with the case packet and the upcoming Yale Case deadline, we wanted to get you the materials as soon as possible. Attached you will find the case materials required for the assignment. As a reminder, cases must be printed and turned in at the start of class. For fairness, anything not on the stack when class begins will be considered late. If you will not be in class, please make arrangements to email your case to me prior to the start of class. Thanks! Gary & Rory

tasks.title: Scriptural Persuasion Account Paper
tasks.description: Please submit here your one page summary and exploration of a scriptural account of someone persuading or convincing. What did you learn?

tasks.title: Revenue Tree Model
tasks.description: Create a revenue tree model for a company of your choice (based on class discussion and the book) Feel free to upload an image, or describe it through text, or draw a representation of the revenue tree with the company listed at the bottom.

tasks.title: Extra Credit Opportunities
tasks.description: Hello Everyone! We are adding here two extra credit opportunities to take advantage of before the end of the semester. First is the book report. We have ten approved titles Influence: Science and Practice Pre-Suasion: A Revolutionary Way to Influence and Persuade The Challenger Sale SPIN Selling Fanatical Prospecting The Sales Development Playbook Never Split the Difference Virtual Selling To Sell is Human The Ultimate Sales Machine Only three students will be allowed to use the same book, so contact me(Stone) as soon as possible to claim your title! Reports should be at least 1000 words in a Word document. They will provide summaries of the book's philosophy, insights, merits and weakness, including a recommendation (or not) to incorporate its ideas. They should also contain a comparison to the philosophy and methodologies in "The Sales Game Changer". Good writing, clarity, and organization of this paper is expected. Book reports are due April 23, 2025. Extra credit points will be a 3% bump in your final grade. This is equal to scoring 30 extra points on one of the quizzes. It should be enough to pull you up if you are close to the grade you want. Extra Credit Opportunity two: For those of you below 100% on participation, this can help you make that up! This is a report on a real-life application of the sales game-changer methodologies. You may repeat this as many times as you desire, to make up as many participation points as you like. This second opportunity is ONLY for making up participation points.

tasks.title: Final Review Document
tasks.description: Hello Everyone. I am attaching here the document from Rob with the 24 prompts, so you can make sure you are ready for the final exam next week. Good luck everybody!
